name: Catch A Lift Fund
desc: Free gym memberships, equipment, coaching, and 8-week wellness program (nutrition + mental health) for post-9/11 combat-injured veterans with 50%+ VA disability rating.

name: Home Base — Warrior Health & Fitness (Florida)
desc: FREE 6-month program — supervised strength training, nutrition education, sleep hygiene, stress management. Up to 60 veterans per cohort. Serving Naples, Fort Myers, and Tampa.

name: Team Red, White & Blue (Team RWB)
desc: 232,000+ member national community. Free weekly fitness events — running, cycling, yoga, functional fitness — plus social events and volunteer projects. Chapters in all 50 states.

name: WarriorWOD
desc: 6-month free gym membership plus virtual nutrition coaching for each veteran. Exercise and nutrition as a prescription for recovery from PTSD and combat stress.

name: Wounded Warrior Project — Physical Health & Wellness
desc: Comprehensive physical health programs including fitness coaching, adaptive sports, nutrition support, and national events for post-9/11 injured veterans.

name: VA Whole Health Program
desc: Holistic wellness at most VA medical centers: health coaching, well-being classes, complementary and integrative health, nutrition, mindfulness, and sleep support.

name: ValorFit
desc: Free or discounted gym access, strength training, and community connections for veterans. Focused on building physical and mental strength together.

name: VA Gerofit Program
desc: VA-supervised exercise program for older veterans, available at select VA medical centers with trained staff and progressive strength/endurance programming.

name: Freedom Fitness America
desc: Fitness services, personal training, nutrition counseling, and wellness programs for veterans and first responders. Community-driven approach to recovery and health.

name: The Resilience Project
desc: Resilience-centered wellness connecting veterans to physical activity, peer support, mindset training, and community-based fitness resources.

name: VA National Veterans Sports Programs
desc: VA-sponsored adaptive sports: National Veterans Wheelchair Games, Winter/Summer Sports Clinics, Golden Age Games, Creative Arts Festival, and TEE Tournament.

name: Paralyzed Veterans of America (PVA) Sports
desc: Wheelchair basketball, rugby, tennis, handcycling, air rifle, and more for paralyzed and spinal cord injured veterans. Chapters nationwide with competitive and recreational options.

name: DoD Warrior Games
desc: Annual multi-sport adaptive competition for wounded, ill, and injured service members and veterans. 13 sports including powerlifting, cycling, swimming, and track & field.

name: BlazeSports America
desc: Adaptive sports programming for veterans and individuals with disabilities. Born from the 1996 Atlanta Paralympics legacy — recreational through competitive levels.

name: Disabled Athlete Sports Association (DASA)
desc: Wheelchair basketball, sled hockey, competitive teams, and recreational adaptive sports for disabled veterans and civilians.

name: God's Love We Deliver — CONNECTED
desc: FREE medically tailored meals for veterans with chronic disease, PTSD, depression, bipolar disorder, or substance use challenges. Personalized by a Registered Dietitian.

name: VA Nutrition & Food Services
desc: VA Registered Dietitian counseling, food security screening, and referrals to community food resources. Available at all VA Medical Centers.

name: USDA Military & Veteran Nutrition Programs
desc: SNAP, WIC, school meals, and other USDA nutrition assistance with military/veteran-specific eligibility guidance and application support.

name: Veterans Collaborative — Food Programs
desc: Online directory connecting veterans to food assistance programs including meal delivery services, food pantries, and nutrition coaching resources.

name: Veterans Yoga Project (VYP)
desc: National nonprofit (est. 2011) partnered with the VA. Free daily classes, Mindful Resilience Training for Trauma Recovery, and yoga teacher training for veteran instructors.

name: Warriors at Ease
desc: Trauma-informed yoga and meditation training for the military community. Certified teachers serve in VA facilities, military installations, and Vet Centers nationwide.

name: VEToga
desc: Free monthly yoga classes, community events, and a veteran-specific 200-hour Teacher Training program. Yoga, meditation, and healing arts for military, veterans, and families.

name: The Mindfulness Center — Veterans Program
desc: Free classes in meditation, yoga, tai chi, and mind-body practices. Individualized acupuncture and mental health counseling for veterans.

name: We Defy Foundation
desc: Free Brazilian Jiu Jitsu and fitness training for combat veterans with military-connected disabilities. Operates across all 50 states, 2 US territories, and Japan.

name: V-MAT (Veterans Martial Arts Training)
desc: 100% free martial arts training for veterans, reservists, and active duty. Accommodates physical disabilities, ADD, PTSD, and TBI.

name: Kobukan Martial Arts — Warriors Program
desc: 100% FREE Jiu-Jitsu, Karate, and MMA training for combat veterans and first responders with PTSD/TBI disability.

name: US Military Endurance Sports (USMES)
desc: Cycling, triathlon, running, and adventure racing for current, retired, and veteran military. 20 regional chapters nationwide with camps, clinics, and wounded veteran adaptive programs.

name: Frontline OCR
desc: Veteran-focused obstacle course racing community. Training programs, team events, and competitive OCR for military and veteran athletes.

name: Project Healing Waters Fly Fishing
desc: Fly fishing, fly tying, and rod building for active-duty military and veterans. Served 6,000+ veterans in 2023. Programs across the USA with 6,500+ volunteers.

name: Veterans Exploration Therapy
desc: Wilderness exploration and therapeutic outdoor recreation. Founded by Iraq/Afghanistan combat veterans. Expeditions nationwide with a trauma-informed approach.

name: Veterans Adventure Group
desc: Extreme outdoor adventures — skydiving, mountaineering, scuba, rock climbing — building veteran communities through shared high-adventure experiences.

name: The Warrior's Keep
desc: Vet-Rec hiking program and Keep Climbing rock climbing program. Reduces stress, anxiety, and depression through outdoor social engagement and peer connection.

name: Warrior Surf Foundation
desc: Free 12-week surf therapy program — yoga and wellness coaching for veterans with PTSD, anxiety, and depression. Includes a psycho-education component.

name: Operation Surf
desc: Curriculum-based surf programs for injured military and veterans. Includes wellness resources, tools, and peer-to-peer support for lasting recovery.

name: Operation Amped
desc: Adaptive surf therapy for seriously ill, injured, and disabled veterans and their families. Therapeutic ocean-based programs in Southern California.

name: PATH International — Equine Programs
desc: National credentialing organization for equine-assisted services. Directory of accredited centers offering veteran therapeutic riding, equine psychotherapy, and physical therapy with horses.

name: Vinceremos — Freedom Reins Program
desc: Freedom Reins therapeutic riding program for veterans in South Florida. Equine-assisted activities focused on veteran healing, confidence-building, and recovery.

name: Veterans Equine Therapeutic Services (V.E.T.S.)
desc: Ground program (horse handling, no riding) plus Mounted program (riding and horsemanship) for veterans. Focused on connection, confidence, and calm.

name: NCEFT — Veterans & First Responders
desc: Clinical hippotherapy, adaptive riding, and veteran programs for PTSD, substance use, and TBI. Peer-to-peer support in a farm environment.

name: Equest — Veterans Program
desc: Equine-assisted activities for veterans in the Dallas/Fort Worth area. Therapeutic riding and equine-facilitated learning for healing and growth.

name: PGA HOPE
desc: Free 6-8 week developmental golf curriculum led by PGA Professionals. Available at 340+ locations in 47 states. All skill levels — no prior golf experience needed.

name: Salute Military Golf Association (SMGA)
desc: Golf for mental and physical rehabilitation of post-9/11 wounded veterans. Co-founded by former PGA Tour player Jim Estes. Community-driven healing through the sport.

name: National Disabled Veterans Golf Clinic
desc: Annual week-long adaptive golf program presented by the VA and DAV. Instruction for veterans with life-changing disabilities — rehabilitation through adaptive golf.

name: Links to Freedom
desc: Adaptive golf taught by PGA and LPGA professionals for injured service members and disabled veterans. Focus on physical, emotional, mental, and social recovery.

name: National Personal Training Institute (NPTI)
desc: VA-approved Personal Trainer diploma program. Accepts GI Bill (Ch 30, 33) and VR&E (Ch 31). Hands-on campus training leading to a career in fitness and personal training.

name: NCSF Certified Strength Coach
desc: Strength Coach certification with GI Bill eligibility. Military Support Plan available for active duty and veteran military. Nationally recognized credential.
